title: Database Access on SEQ
text: Database Access on SEQ Logon Path Paths (for example, in assignment files) should be specified only for files. Logon information is not required. The default extension for sequential files is .udf . You can assign an entity by entering the following line in the [ENTITIES] section of your assignment file: Entity .udf = $seq The following line informs Uniface where the sequential file can be found, and then correctly assigns the entity: Entity.Model = $seq: Entity .udf Open/Close Support The open and close ProcScript commands are not supported. If they are issued, the SEQ connector does nothing. Using SEQ for the Repository You can use the SEQ data connector for Uniface system information in a production environment, as long as you are aware of the limitations stated in Data Retrieval on SEQ . Accessing Multiple Databases Not applicable. The sequential connector creates and accesses files, not tables. I/O Channels One I/O channel is open for each open file. Was this topic helpful?
